canonical_filename.mechanism: Fixed filename SKILL.md required in each skill directory. All scopes (workspace, global, system) use this same convention.

description.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: description (required). Brief explanation shown to the model to help it decide when to auto-invoke the skill. Key field for automatic invocation matching.

display_name.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: name (required). Must be lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only (e.g., deploy-to-staging, code-review). Used in UI listings and for @-mentions.

global_scope.mechanism: Skill directory at ~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/<skill-name>/. Available in all workspaces on the user's machine; not committed to version control. Also discovered at ~/.agents/skills/<skill-name>/ and (if Claude Code config reading is enabled) ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/.

project_scope.mechanism: Skill directory at .windsurf/skills/<skill-name>/. Committed with the repo; current workspace only. For cross-agent compatibility, also discovered at .agents/skills/<skill-name>/ and (if Claude Code config reading is enabled) .claude/skills/<skill-name>/.

shared_scope.mechanism: OS-specific system path deployed by enterprise IT; read-only for end users. macOS: /Library/Application Support/Windsurf/skills/<skill-name>/; Linux/WSL: /etc/windsurf/skills/<skill-name>/; Windows: C:\ProgramData\Windsurf\skills\<skill-name>\.

user_invocable.mechanism: Explicit @mention by skill name in Cascade input (e.g., @deploy-to-production). Always available regardless of auto-invocation eligibility.

providerExtensions.name: Supporting resource files
providerExtensions.description: A skill directory may contain arbitrary additional files alongside SKILL.md (e.g., shell scripts, markdown checklists, config templates, test templates, environment files). These files become available to Cascade when the skill is invoked. There is no restriction on file type or count. The skill directory is effectively a mini-bundle of everything needed to execute a multi-step workflow. This is the primary structural differentiator between Skills (folder-based bundles) and Rules/Workflows (single-file). Examples from docs: deployment-checklist.md, rollback-procedure.md, config-template.yaml, pre-deploy-checks.sh, security-checklist.md, review-template.md, test-template.py, coverage-config.json.

providerExtensions.name: Skill name format constraint
providerExtensions.description: Skill names (the `name` frontmatter field) must be unique per scope and use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. This constraint is enforced by the UI wizard and documented as a requirement. Invalid names are not accepted. Examples of valid names: deploy-to-staging, code-review, setup-dev-environment.

providerExtensions.name: Automatic model-driven invocation
providerExtensions.description: Beyond explicit @mention, Cascade automatically invokes skills when the user's request semantically matches a skill's description. This is described as the most common invocation path. The description frontmatter field is the key signal: it tells Cascade when to invoke the skill. This is distinct from user_invocable (@mention) and is driven entirely by model inference against the description field. Users simply describe what they want and Cascade selects relevant skills without any explicit invocation syntax.

providerExtensions.name: UI-based skill creation
providerExtensions.description: Skills can be created through the Cascade panel UI without manual file creation. Flow: open Cascade panel → three dots (top right) → customizations menu → Skills section → click "+ Workspace" (project-specific) or "+ Global" (all workspaces) → enter name. The UI enforces the name format constraint (lowercase, numbers, hyphens).
